comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Root-caused during c7c5 dogfooding: the over-exploration/non-convergence was ToolStrategy's forced tool_choice preventing natural termination (A/B: >250 tool calls WITH it / timeout vs ~17 WITHOUT, same model/prompt/ticket; a Claude Code sonnet subagent: ~12) — NOT AutoStrategy per se. The completion verifier now uses RunRequest.output_strategy='extract' (run with no forced response_format -> natural termination in text -> tool-less with_structured_output extraction). This ticket remains: should review/code/scan also move off ToolStrategy (to 'extract' or AutoStrategy/ProviderStrategy) framework-wide? Prior-art: forcing the loop is the documented anti-pattern; bigger/reasoning models over-explore MORE.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Doc-hygiene done (comment/doc text only, no runtime change). DECISION: AutoStrategy/ProviderStrategy spike is moot — superseded by the pydantic_ai runtime cutover; no migration needed. Verified no forced-tool strategy classes exist: grep -rE 'ToolStrategy|AutoStrategy|ProviderStrategy' src/rebar returns zero matches. Edits: (1) runner.py RunRequest.thinking comment (~l67-78) — reframed PromptedOutput-on-thinking as a CURRENT Anthropic-400 API constraint, not a forced-tool workaround; (2) runner.py PydanticAIRunner docstring (~l148-151, surfaced by plan-review advisory) — output is selected by output_mode() (NativeOutput/PromptedOutput, no forced-tool ToolOutput); corrected stale 'reliability hardening layered on in a later story (1268)' to present-tense (1268 is shipped); (3) structured.py module docstring (~l9-15) — relabelled the thinking+ToolOutput-400 note as a live API constraint on current pydantic_ai output modes, not a relic of a removed forced-tool mechanism; (4) structured.py output_mode() docstring (~l52-56) — same relabel. Preserved the genuinely-still-true Anthropic-400 (thinking + native/forced constraint) rationale and the PromptedOutput-choice reasoning per plan-review advisories fc8dbf/f6ec7b. make lint clean; pytest -k 'runner or structured' 116 passed/2 skipped; pytest -k llm 125 passed/11 skipped.

compiled_state.description:
## Status (rescoped 2026-06-29)
The spike's functional goal is MOOT. The framework already moved off forced-tool `ToolStrategy` entirely: the pydantic_ai runtime cutover means review/code/scan + the completion verifier all run on natural-termination output via `output_mode()` (`PromptedOutput`/`NativeOutput`) — there is no `ToolStrategy`/`AutoStrategy`/`ProviderStrategy` anywhere in `src/rebar`. So the BI-1 optional-None-leakage class the spike worried about is already structurally avoided, and an AutoStrategy/ProviderStrategy evaluation is no longer worth running.

What remains is pure DOC HYGIENE: the now-stale "disable extended thinking because forced tool-choice conflicts" rationale still lingers in the runner/structured-output source comments and reads as if forced-tool is still in use.

## Scope
- Update/remove the stale "disable thinking (forced tool-choice conflict)" rationale in `src/rebar/llm/runner.py` (~lines 72-77) and `src/rebar/llm/structured.py` (~lines 11-12, 52-54) so it reflects the current pydantic_ai `PromptedOutput`/`NativeOutput` reality (keep any genuinely-still-true note as historical context, clearly labelled).
- Record the decision in-ticket: the AutoStrategy/ProviderStrategy spike is superseded by the pydantic_ai cutover; no migration needed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Stale forced-tool/"disable thinking" rationale in `runner.py` + `structured.py` is corrected or removed (no comment implies forced `ToolStrategy` is the current mechanism).
- [ ] Decision recorded: spike moot — superseded by the pydantic_ai runtime cutover (cite that no ToolStrategy/AutoStrategy/ProviderStrategy exists in `src/rebar`).
- [ ] `make lint` + test suite pass.

file_impact.reason: Correct stale forced-tool/disable-thinking comment rationale (RunRequest fields + PydanticAIRunner docstring)
Correct stale forced-tool framing; preserve live Anthropic-400 constraint as current reality
